response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
Report the two named records in alphabetical order, then report the dependent action or comparison.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update Cooking with Winter Roots only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and on-loan status.

arguments.body: Cooking with Winter Roots was updated to on-hold after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Cooking with Winter Roots

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record lib-673 changes from on-loan to on-hold.
Record lib-273 and distractor lib-1073 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to circulation desk, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve The Quiet Observatory in Central Branch and Cooking with Winter Roots in East Branch with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Cooking with Winter Roots is still on-loan, change only Cooking with Winter Roots from on-loan to on-hold. Leave The Quiet Observatory unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify circulation desk with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Cooking with Winter Roots was updated to on-hold after status verification.
